working_on: Fix Issues #122/#123/#124 — routine-mode doc drift (start-routine.md thin-pointer conversion, Skill/ops-doc step-10 reword) + MemPalace boot-timing soft-warns.
declared_scope: Reword routine-mode-lifecycle SKILL + routine-mode-operations.md step 10 and add a routine_lifecycle_push.py docstring note (#122); rewrite .claude/commands/start-routine.md as a thin pointer to the routine-mode-lifecycle Skill (#123); add per-tool first-call timestamps to scan_mempalace_activity.py and two mempalace_*_late soft-warns to validate.py with taxonomy-doc + test coverage (#124). phase: NA-engine-apparatus
outcome_summary: Fixed Issues #122/#123/#124 (common root: start-routine.md drifted into a stale subset of the canonical routine procedure). #123: rewrote start-routine.md as a thin pointer to the routine-mode-lifecycle Skill. #122: reworded SKILL+ops-doc step 10 (stage, not standalone-commit) + routine_lifecycle_push.py docstring. #124: per-tool first-call timestamps in scan_mempalace_activity.py + mempalace_boot_query_late/diary_read_late soft-warns in validate.py, backward-compatible. 17 new tests; 1455/1455 pytest; validate.py --final-check 0 hard-fails, 48 checks. No ADR (existing contracts ADR 0044/0051/0056). Filed follow-up Issue #125 (/start-engine.md thin-pointer conversion + session-build-lifecycle SKILL parity prerequisite). First natural exercise of the #124 mechanism: this session's own diary read ran ~1 min after started_at, so the close fires mempalace_diary_read_late as expected — accurate telemetry, not a defect.
